2605.24971.title: TGFormer: Towards Temporal Graph Transformer with Auto-Correlation Mechanism
2605.24971.paper_summary: The growing interest in Temporal Graph Neural Networks (TGNNs) stems from their ability to model complex dynamics and deliver superior performance. However, TGNNs encounter fundamental challenges in capturing long-term dependencies and identifying periodic patterns. To address these limitations, we propose TGFormer, a novel Transformer architecture specifically designed for temporal graphs. Our model redefines temporal graph learning by establishing a trajectory framework that aligns with time series analysis principles. This approach allows TGFormer to derive node representations through systematic analysis of historical interactions, enabling granular examination of node relationships across sequential timestamps. Building upon stochastic process theory, we develop an auto-correlation mechanism that systematically uncovers periodic dependencies in node interactions. This innovation empowers TGFormer to perform dependency discovery and representation aggregation at sub-interaction levels, demonstrating superior efficiency and accuracy compared to conventional attention mechanisms. Experimental validation across six public benchmarks confirms the effectiveness of our approach, with TGFormer at most achieving 9.35\% precision improvement compared to state-of-the-art approaches.
2605.24971.paper_summary_zh: 隨著對時間圖神經網絡（TGNNs）興趣的增長，源於其建模複雜動態和提供卓越性能的能力。<lb/>然而，TGNNs在捕捉長期依賴性和識別周期性模式方面面臨根本挑戰。<lb/>為了應對這些限制，我們提出了TGFormer，一種專為時間圖設計的新型Transformer架構。<lb/>我們的模型通過建立與時間序列分析原則一致的軌跡框架，重新定義了時間圖學習。<lb/>這種方法使TGFormer能夠通過系統分析歷史交互來推導節點表示，從而能夠對跨連續時間戳的節點關係進行細緻的檢查。<lb/>基於隨機過程理論，我們開發了一種自相關機制，系統性地揭示節點交互中的周期性依賴性。<lb/>這一創新使TGFormer能夠在子交互層級執行依賴性發現和表示聚合，顯示出比傳統注意力機制更優越的效率和準確性。<lb/>在六個公共基準上的實驗驗證確認了我們方法的有效性，TGFormer在大多數情況下相比於最先進的方法實現了9.35\%的精度提升。
2605.24971.author: Hongjiang Chen et.al.
2605.24971.authors: Hongjiang Chen, Pengfei Jiao, Ming Du, Xuan Guo, Zhidong Zhao, Di Jin, Xiao Liu
